com.github.dakusui.pcond.functions

Enum Functions

  • java.lang.Object
    • java.lang.Enum<Functions>
      • com.github.dakusui.pcond.functions.Functions
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    Serializable, Comparable<Functions>

    public enum Functions
extends Enum<Functions>

    An entry point for acquiring function objects. Functions retrieved by method in this class are all "printable".

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Static Methods Concrete Methods

      Returns a function that casts an object into a given class.

    • Method Detail

      • values

        public static Functions[] values()
        Returns an array containing the constants of this enum type, in the order they are declared. This method may be used to iterate over the constants as follows: 
        for (Functions c : Functions.values())
    System.out.println(c);
        Returns:
        an array containing the constants of this enum type, in the order they are declared

      • valueOf

        public static Functions valueOf(String name)
        Returns the enum constant of this type with the specified name. The string must match exactly an identifier used to declare an enum constant in this type. (Extraneous whitespace characters are not permitted.)
        Parameters:
        name - the name of the enum constant to be returned.
        Returns:
        the enum constant with the specified name
        Throws:
        IllegalArgumentException - if this enum type has no constant with the specified name
        NullPointerException - if the argument is null

        Type Parameters:

        E - Type of the elements in the collection

      • curry

        public static CurriedFunction<Object,Object> curry(Class<?> aClass,
                                                   String methodName,
                                                   Class<?>... parameterTypes)

        Curries a static method specified by the given arguments.

        Parameters:
        aClass - A class to which the method to be curried belongs to.
        methodName - A name of the method to be curried.
        parameterTypes - Parameters types of the method.
        Returns:
        A printable and curried function of the target method.

        Parameters:
        function - A multi-function to be curried
        Returns:
        A curried function

        public static <R> MultiFunction<R> multifunction(Class<?> aClass,

                                                 Class<?>... parameterTypes)

      • call

        public static <T,R> Function<T,R> call(MethodQuery methodQuery)

        Returns a Function created from a method specified by a methodQuery. If the methodQuery matches none or more than one methods, a RuntimeException will be thrown.

        Type Parameters:
        T - the type of the input to the returned function
        Parameters:
        methodQuery - A query object that specifies a method to be invoked by the returned function.
        Returns:
        Created function.
        See Also:
        classMethod(Class, String, Object[]), instanceMethod(Object, String, Object[])

      • classMethod

        public static MethodQuery classMethod(Class<?> targetClass,
                                      String methodName,
                                      Object... arguments)

        Creates a MethodQuery object from given arguments to search for static methods. Note that arguments are actual argument values, not the formal parameter types. The pcond library searches for the "best" matching method for you. In case no matching method is found or more than one methods are found, a RuntimeException will be thrown.

        In order to specify a parameter which should be passed to the returned function at applying, you can use an object returned by parameter() method. This is useful to construct a function from an existing method.

        That is, in order to create a function which computes sin using query a method Math.sin(double), you can do following

        public void buildSinFunction() {
  MethodQuery mq = classMethod(Math.class, "sin", parameter());
  Function<Double, Double> sin = call(mq);
  System.out.println(sin(Math.PI/2));
}

        This prints 1.0.

        In case your arguments do not contain any Parameter object, the input argument passed to the built function will be simply ignored.

        Parameters:
        targetClass - A class
        methodName - A method name
        arguments - Arguments
        Returns:
        A method query for static methods specified by arguments.
        See Also:
        ReflUtils.findMethod(Class, String, Object[])

      • instanceMethod

        public static MethodQuery instanceMethod(Object targetObject,
                                         String methodName,
                                         Object... arguments)

        Creates a MethodQuery object from given arguments to search for static methods. Excepting that this method returns a query for instance methods, it is quite similar to classMethod( Class, String, Object[]).

        This method is useful to build a function from an instance method. That is, you can create a function which returns the length of a given string from a method String.length() with a following code snippet.

        public void buildLengthFunction() {
  Function<String, Integer> length = call(instanceMethod(parameter(), "length"));
}

        In case the targetObject is not an instance of Parameter and arguments contain no Parameter object, the function will simply ignore the input passed to it.

        Parameters:
        targetObject - An object on which methods matching returned query should be invoked.
        methodName - A name of method.
        arguments - Arguments passed to the method.
        Returns:
        A method query for instance methods specified by arguments.
        See Also:
        classMethod(Class, String, Object[])

      • parameter

        public static Parameter parameter()

        Returns a Parameter object, which is used in combination with instanceMethod(Object, String, Object[]) or classMethod(Class, String, Object[]). This object is replaced with the actual input value passed to a function built through call(MethodQuery) or Predicates.callp(MethodQuery) when it is applied.

        Returns:
        a Parameter object

      • chain

        public static <T,R> Function<T,R> chain(String methodName,
                                        Object... arguments)

        Returns a function that calls a method which matches the given methodName and args on the object given as input to it. <p> Note that method look up is done when the function is applied. <p> Note that method look up is done when the predicate is applied. This means this method does not throw any exception by itself and in case you give wrong methodName or arguments, an exception will be thrown when the returned function is applied.

        Type Parameters:
        T - The type of input to the returned function
        R - The type of output from the returned function
        Parameters:
        methodName - The method name
        arguments - Arguments passed to the method.
        Returns:
        A function that invokes the method matching the methodName and args

      • chainp

        public static <T> Predicate<T> chainp(String methodName,
                                      Object... arguments)

        Returns a predicate that calls a method which matches the given methodName and args on the object given as input to it. <p> Note that method look up is done when the predicate is applied. This means this method does not throw any exception by itself and in case you give wrong methodName or arguments, an exception will be thrown when the returned function is applied.

        Type Parameters:
        T - The type of input to the returned predicate
        Parameters:
        methodName - The method name
        arguments - Arguments passed to the method.
        Returns:
        A predicate that invokes the method matching the methodName and args
